source_jobs.title: IT Engineer, Cashalo
source_jobs.description:
JOIN THE TILT TEAM

At Tilt, we see a side of people that traditional lenders miss. Our mobile-first products and machine learning-powered credit models look beyond credit scores, using over 250 real-time financial signals to recognize real potential. With millions of customers worldwide, we're not just changing how people access financial products — we're creating a new credit system that backs the working, whatever they're working toward.


THE OPPORTUNITY: IT ENGINEER, CASHALO

We are looking for a IT Engineer to be the hands-on face of IT in our Manila office. You'll be the first responder for everything physical and local — laptops, desks, cables, office internet, access — keeping Cashalo's people productive and the office running while working within the standards set by Tilt Global IT.

You'll report to the Tilt Global IT Manager and work day to day alongside Cashalo's engineering and people teams. Global IT designs the infrastructure; you bring it to life on the ground.

This role is office based, in our Manila HQ.





HOW YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT

 - Keep people moving - Onboard and offboard laptops, resolve hardware issues (RAM, viruses, repairs), and provide first line support so employees are never blocked

 - Deploy on the ground - Install and deploy hardware and network assets per Global IT's designs: racking equipment, connecting cables, standing up new desks and rooms

 - Own local connectivity - Troubleshoot office internet issues, manage GSM SIM rotation, and engage cellular vendors to keep agent operations online

 - Be the escalation point - Provide on call tier 1 support and coordinate local vendors for repairs, deliveries, and site work

 - Protect the office - Provision office security access and monitor access logs, flagging anomalies to Global IT

 - Support the bigger picture - Be Global IT's hands and eyes on site, assisting with centrally led projects that need local execution
   
   


WHY YOU'RE A GREAT FIT

 - You've done hands-on IT support before - laptops, printers, Wi-Fi, cabling - and you enjoy fixing things quickly and properly

 - You're reliable under pressure: when someone can't work, you treat it with urgency

 - You follow standards and documentation carefully, and speak up when something on the ground doesn't match the plan

 - You communicate clearly with non-technical colleagues and with a remote central team

 - You're comfortable coordinating local vendors and holding them to deadlines

 - You thrive in fast paced, lean environments

Don't meet every qualification? We care about potential over your past. If you're bringing ambition and drive to what we're building, we want to hear from you.



Don’t meet every qualification? We care about potential over your past. If you're bringing ambition and drive to what we're building, we want to hear from you.

 - Competitive pay: We're big on potential, and it's reflected in our competitive compensation packages and generous equity.

 - Complete support: Find flexible health plans at every premium level, and substantial subsidies that stand up to global standards.

THE OPPORTUNITY: AGENTIC AI LEAD (BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION)

Tilt is an AI-native lender moving quickly from early experimentation to full integration across our global functions. The Agentic AI Lead will partner with senior leaders to define Tilt’s future operating system as we progress in this journey.

This critical role sits at the intersection of workforce reskilling and organizational transformation — leading how Tilt designs, pilots, and scales AI-powered ways of working across the company.




HOW YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT

 - Design Tilt’s future operating systems: Partner with senior leaders to diagnose current pain points and co-design AI-first processes and systems that scale with the business.

 - Shape the “agentic” operating model: Define how Tilt integrates autonomous tools, human judgment, and systemized knowledge into everyday decision-making.

 - Drive measurable outcomes: Deliver technical and analytical projects that unlock speed, reduce manual effort, and increase team capacity.

 - Lead proof-of-concepts: Prototype and validate redesigned processes using tools like Claude, N8N, and custom GPTs/skills.

 - Translate innovation into scale: Convert proof-of-concept learnings into scalable frameworks and playbooks that can be applied across functions.

 - Enable technical transformation: Collaborate with data and engineering partners to ensure our infrastructure supports demand for agentic capabilities.

 - Advise leadership: Act as a trusted advisor to the CEO, Head of Product, and senior leaders, aligning AI capabilities with business priorities.

 - Champion cultural change: Promote new ways of working that preserve Tilt’s culture of ownership while accelerating responsible AI adoption.


WHY YOU'RE A GREAT FIT

 - 8+ years of experience in business transformation, product strategy, or technical operations — ideally in high-growth or complex environments as a founder or founding/early hire

 - Proven success leading cross-functional initiatives that reimagine systems or workflows.

 - Strong technical literacy with mainstream and cutting edge AI tools and automation platforms (e.g., Claude Code/Codex/Gemini, N8N, OpenClaw, Replit).

 - Hands-on experience building or deploying workflow automations, agents, or custom skills.

 - Excellent communicator with the ability to build trust and alignment across technical and non-technical teams.

 - Systems thinker with a bias for execution — able to connect people, process, and technology to deliver impact.

 - Bachelor’s degree in a technical or quantitative field (Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, etc.); MBA a plus.

THE OPPORTUNITY: FOUNDING PRODUCT MANAGER, NIRA



NIRA is an established and growing fintech lender in India, and product is central to where we’re headed next. We are compounding fast: funnel improvements are landing, we are building toward our own NBFC, and our next credit product is moving from strategy into build. This is the first PM hire in NIRA’s current team.



India's financial infrastructure spanning Account Aggregator, Credit Line on UPI, the Unified Lending Interface is advancing faster than anywhere in the world. UPI registers nearly a billion transactions a day. You’ll be building a product on top of infrastructure that doesn’t exist at this scale anywhere else, and the window to shape how it’s used in lending is right now.



As part of a small, high-ownership team on the ground in Bangalore, you will be part of an international product and leadership network with direct accountability for outcomes that matter.

 

How You’ll Make an Impact

 - Drive strategy through deep user insight: Know the borrower better than anyone else by combining continuous, hands-on qualitative research with hard data. You will translate these ground-truth learnings directly into foundational product direction and nuanced UX decisions.

 - Own the lending funnel end-to-end: Own the lending funnel as a product system. Develop a point of view on where the highest-leverage interventions sit (UX, policy, pricing, segmentation), prioritize ruthlessly, and be accountable for moving conversion, approval, and disbursal — including the willingness to kill work that isn't moving the needle

 - Build Short Term Credit from the ground up: Own the 0-to-1 product strategy by defining the core hypothesis, segmenting the target user base, and identifying the critical early use cases to win. From this strategic foundation, architect the end-to-end borrower experience. Lead the continuous refinement of UX in lockstep with credit policy for this fast-moving, high-frequency product.

 - Own strategy and roadmap: Own product strategy and roadmap, translating business goals and customer needs into clear priorities, initiatives, and measurable outcomes.Regularly produce work that moves company-level OKRs. Defend prioritisation decisions with data.

 - Work with AI tools the way great practitioners do: We use Claude and other AI tools actively in our day-to-day. You’ll use them to compress the time from hypothesis to validated signal — specs, analysis, prototyping, discovery. You see AI as a force multiplier on personal output, not a novelty.

 - You act, you don’t wait: You make the call when information is 70% complete and the cost of waiting exceeds the cost of being wrong. You don’t escalate solvable problems. Seniority here means fewer gates, not more.

 - Quantitative by default: Data is not a backup for intuition - it’s the starting point. You run your own queries, build your own models, and produce consistently error-free analysis. You change your position in response to data without needing a second opinion to give you permission.

 - Resource-constraint native: You cut scope to preserve core intent - optimising for low bandwidth, not more headcount.

 - Systems thinker: You reason in stocks and flows; you build explicit models, name their assumptions, and identify which assumption is most likely to break.

source_jobs.description:
JOIN THE TILT TEAM

With millions of customers worldwide, we're not just changing how people access financial products — we're creating a new credit system that backs the working, whatever they're working toward.



THE OPPORTUNITY: SENIOR MOBILE ENGINEER, THRIVE

The Thrive team owns Tilt's line of credit products — the financial tools that help users grow their purchasing power and build stronger credit standing over time. We run two products: Thrive, a credit-building line of credit that can grow to $1,200+ through responsible use and reports directly to credit bureaus; and cash advance plus, which gives users flexible access to funds when they need them most. These aren't experimental bets — they're live products with real users, real compliance obligations, and real room to grow.



Our next chapter is expanding the reach of these products and building towards a unified product experience: eliminating the seams between our offerings so users move through one seamless journey, getting the right product for their situation without having to think about it. Getting there requires validating the right product constructs through focused experimentation before committing to full implementation. You'll own lead that work on iOS, along with the team.



The team is lean and high-ownership — a staff iOS engineer, an Android engineer, and you. This is a net-new role created by expanded investment in both products simultaneously, not a backfill. Within 6–12 months, as our staff iOS engineer takes on broader responsibilities, you'll work on and own the full iOS surface for these products.



Travel for company offsites is expected at a minimum 2 times a year.




HOW YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT

 - Drive the growth for these line of credit products: Own the iOS experience across our credit products — optimizing onboarding, application conversion rates, improving repeat usage , and continuously raising the bar on how users move from application to active product use.

 - Run experiments that advance the unified product vision: Test different product constructs and UX approaches that move us toward a single, seamless lending experience. You'll work from hypothesis to launch, validating what actually works before we scale it.

 - Operate at the outcome level: You'll be trusted to an outcome — say, 40% conversion — and expected to improve it through your own problem-solving. That means working with design and product to identify the right levers, not waiting for a spec that tells you exactly what to build.

 - Hold the line on precision in a regulated product: Errors in credit products don't just create bugs — they trigger compliance requirements: corrective notices, potential fee refunds, regulatory exposure. Your attention to detail is as much a product requirement as your engineering skill.

 - Grow into full iOS ownership: Work alongside a staff iOS engineer charting a path to onboarding into and owning iOS for one or both of these products within 6–12 months — this is a real and near-term expansion of scope.

 - Collaborate across the full stack: Partner with PMs, designers, data science, and backend engineers to build credit experiences that hold up at every layer — technically, experientially, and regulatory.




WHY YOU'RE A GREAT FIT

 - 5+ years of experience as an iOS engineer building consumer-facing mobile products. You're strong enough to own technical tradeoffs and quality without close oversight.

 - Consumer product background is non-negotiable. You've built products that real users interact with daily. Candidates who are primarily interested on iOS infrastructure (e.g., app load time, build systems) or internal tooling might not be the right fit for this role.

At Tilt, we don’t just see numbers on a spreadsheet; we see the people behind them. As The People’s Credit Company, we’re on a mission to kick down the doors of traditional finance. To do that, we need more than just technical expertise—we need a Senior Data Scientist who blends analytical grit with deep emotional intelligence.

You’ll join our Methodologies group, the team responsible for building the foundational models that power our "people-first" philosophy. While others might play it safe with legacy credit scoring, you’ll be in the trenches using deep learning to find the truth in financial behavior. You’ll explore how neural networks, embeddings, and transformers can turn complex data into fair, accessible credit for everyone.

As a Senior member of the team, you are a high-impact contributor who has moved beyond executing tasks to mastering the influence of the work. You are a bridge-builder—bold enough to challenge the status quo and thoughtful enough to bring your cross-functional partners along with you.




HOW YOU'LL MAKE AN IMPACT

 - Architecting the Future: You won't just run experiments; you’ll own the development and testing of deep learning architectures (transformers, embeddings, and NNs) that define how Tilt understands risk.

 - Driving Strategy Across Teams: You’ll partner with peers in Engineering and Product to ensure our models aren't just "smart" in a lab, but effective and scalable in the real world.

 - Translating Insights into Solutions: You’ll look past the symptoms of a data problem to find the root cause. You’ll define trade-offs and turn complex findings into clear, actionable solutions for our lending platform.

 - Owning the Workstream: With general direction from leadership, you will take full ownership of your projects—from initial hypothesis to measuring the outcome for our customers.

 - Elevating the Craft: You’ll share your findings and mentor others, helping the team grow its deep learning capability and ensuring we stay at the cutting edge of the industry.

 - Measuring What Matters: You’ll define success not just by model accuracy, but by how your work improves financial outcomes for Tilt’s customers and partners.




WHY YOU'RE A GREAT FIT

 - Technical Depth: You have a proven track record of building and deploying deep learning models (Neural Networks, Embeddings, or Transformers) using modern frameworks like PyTorch or TensorFlow.

 - Applied Experience: You’ve applied machine learning to complex, real-world problems, ideally within financial services (credit, lending, or payments).

 - First Principles Thinking: You are an expert at breaking down problems. You don't just apply a model because it's popular; you apply it because you’ve considered the trade-offs and the "why."

 - Unwavering Clarity: You cut through the noise. You can explain a complex technical concept to a non-technical partner with the same ease and respect you’d use with a fellow scientist.

 - Soulful Intent: You’re driven by a purpose. You want your intelligence to serve a mission—helping more people get the "Yes" they deserve.

We don’t believe in hand-holding; we believe in partnership. Your benchmark for success is defining the path forward rather than waiting for a roadmap.

The job ahead isn't building the warehouse; it's scaling how we extend it so Analytics, Data Science, and app can move fast without bottlenecking on us. But the defining trait of this role is broader: you will lead the operational work that doesn't touch the customer directly, whatever form it takes, and oversee our 30+ workloads currently managed by the team.

You will manage team leads and the analysts beneath them, set the operating rhythm across every workload the function carries, and own the metrics the function is measured on.  - Regulatory defensibility: Hold the function to a standard that withstands audit and regulatory exams, partnering with Legal and Compliance on FCRA, Reg Z, Reg E, SCRA, and related obligations.
